---
title: "(4) Markdown"
---

> **Acknowledgements:** This page is adapted from [health-data-science-OR/jupyterlab-introduction](https://github.com/health-data-science-OR/jupyterlab-introduction/blob/main/01_basic_markdown.ipynb).

## What is markdown?

Markdown is a **way of writing documents** using plain text plus a few extra characters to show structure and formatting. Instead of a hidden file format (like a `.docx`), everything is visible as text: your words and the little symbols (e.g., `#`, `*`, `-`) that control how they look.

When you *view* or *render* a Markdown file (for example, by opening it in a preview window or creating a final document), a tool reads your text and these symbols and turns them into a nicely formatted document (for example, HTML or PDF).

A normal Markdown file usually ends in `.md`. In this workshop we mostly used **Quarto Markdown**, which is the same idea but with extra features for things like interactivity, code and citations, and those files end in `.qmd`.

On this page you will practice Markdown features that you can use in your Quarto documents.

## Headings and subheadings

In Markdown, a heading is formatted with the character `#`.

The number of `#` characters represents the level of the heading. For example:

```markdown
# Heading level 1

## Heading level 2

### Heading level 3

#### Heading level 4
```

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** Open `markdown.qmd` and create three headings:

* [ ] A level 1 heading for the page title (for example, `# My Markdown Page`).
* [ ] A level 2 heading called `## Section One`.
* [ ] A level 3 heading called `### A Smaller Section`.

Render the page and check that the heading sizes look different.

:::

## Adding a horizontal line between sections

To add a horizontal line between sections, use three dashes or asterisk on a line by themselves - either:

```markdown
---
***
```

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** In `markdown.qmd`:

* [ ] Add a horizontal line between two sections (for example, between your `Section One` and `A Smaller Section` headings)

Re-render the page to check it has worked.

:::

## Bold and italic text

To create **bold** text, you need to surround a word or phrase with `**`. For example:

```markdown
The final word of this sentence will be **bold**.
```

To create *italic* text, you need to surround a word or phrase with `*`.

```markdown
*This whole sentence will be in italics.*
```

You can also **combine *both*** if you need stronger emphasis.

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** In `markdown.qmd`:

* [ ] Write one short sentence about anything you like (e.g., "I am learning Quarto today").
* [ ] Make one word in the sentence **bold**.
* [ ] Make a different word in the sentence *italic*.

Re-render the page to see the effect.

:::

## Bullet points and numbered lists

To create a bullet list, use `*` followed by a space and then your text:

```markdown
* Bullet point 1
* Bullet point 2
* Bullet point 3
```

You can indent with spaces (or tab) to create sub-bullets:

```markdown
* Bullet point 1
    * Bullet point level 2
        * Bullet point level 3
* Back to level 1
```

A numbered list is created in a similar way using numbers (or letters):

```markdown
1. Numbered point 1
    a. Numbered point level 2 
    b. Numbered point level 2 again
2. Back to level 1
```

To create a checklist, add `[ ]` between the bullet and text. You can then click on the boxes to cross them out, or have them already shown as crossed out using `[x]`.

```markdown
* [ ] First task
* [x] Finished task
```

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** In `markdown.qmd`:

* [ ] Create a bullet list of 3 everyday items (e.g., `Tea`, `Coffee`, `Biscuits`).
* [ ] Create a numbered list of 3 simple steps (e.g., `Open the file`, `Make a change`, `Save and render`).
* [ ] Add a checklist with three tasks, and mark one as already completed (e.g., `Read one article`, `Write one paragraph of notes`, `Add article to a report`).

:::

## Indented text

To indent text as a block quote, use `>` at the start of the line:

```markdown
> This is indented
```

You can use this for quotes, highlights, or short notes.

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** In `markdown.qmd`:

* [ ] Add a short quote using `>`, for example `> Quarto helps me keep my work organised.`

:::

## Callouts

Quarto extends Markdown with **callouts**, which are highlighted boxes you can use for notes, tips, or warnings.

Callouts use a small bit of extra syntax that Quarto understands as a "special box" rather than normal text. In Markdown, `::: ... :::` defines a block (a kind of container) that Quarto treats like an HTML `<div>`. By adding a class to that block, you tell Quarto what kind of callout it is.

A simple callout note looks like this:

* `:::` starts the block.
* `{.callout-note}` says that this is a class "callout-note" (just like HTML `<div class="callout-note">`)/
* Everything until the closing `:::` is the content of the callout.
* The final `:::` closes the block.

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=60%}

Markdown:

```markdown
::: {.callout-note}
This is a callout.
You can use it to draw attention to important information.
:::
```

::::

:::: {.column width=5%}

::::

:::: {.column width=35%}

Output:

::: {.callout-note}
This is a callout.
You can use it to draw attention to important information.
:::

::::

:::::

You can change the type:

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=60%}

```markdown
::: {.callout-tip}
This is a tip.
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.callout-warning}
This is a warning.
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.callout-important}
This is important information.
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.callout-caution}
This is a caution.
:::
```

::::

:::: {.column width=5%}

::::

:::: {.column width=35%}

::: {.callout-tip}
This is a tip.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
This is a warning.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
This is important information.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
This is a caution.
:::

::::

:::::

You can add a title:

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=60%}

```markdown
::: {.callout-tip title="Helpful tip"}
Remember to save your file before you render.
:::
```

::::

:::: {.column width=5%}

::::

:::: {.column width=35%}

::: {.callout-tip title="Helpful tip"}
Remember to save your file before you render.
:::

::::

:::::

You can also set the callout to collapse:

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=60%}

```
::: {.callout-note title="More details" collapse="true"}
This extra information is hidden until you click the arrow.
:::
```

::::

:::: {.column width=5%}

::::

:::: {.column width=35%}

::: {.callout-note title="More details" collapse="true"}
This extra information is hidden until you click the arrow.
:::

::::

:::::

You can change the appearance style to `simple` or `minimal`:

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=60%}

```markdown
::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This is a simple callout.
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
This is a minimal callout.
:::
```

::::

:::: {.column width=5%}

::::

:::: {.column width=35%}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This is a simple callout.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
This is a minimal callout.
:::

::::

:::::

You can also hide the icon:

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=60%}

```markdown
::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
Default callout with icon removed
:::
```

::::

:::: {.column width=5%}

::::

:::: {.column width=35%}

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
Default callout with icon removed
:::

::::

:::::

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** In `markdown.qmd`:

* [ ] Add a callout of type `tip` with a short title (e.g., "Helpful tip").
* [ ] Inside the callout, write one sentence of advice (e.g., "Take short breaks when you are concentrating.").
* [ ] Add another callout with `collapse="true"` (e.g., with title "More information" and with content "You can come back to this note later if you need it.").

:::

## Line break

You can insert a line break using `<br>`.

```markdown
First part of the sentence.<br>Second part on a new line.
```

::: {.pale-blue}

**Task:** In `markdown.qmd`:

* [ ] Write a short sentence, then use `<br>` to put a second short sentence on the next line with a line break between them.

:::

## Hyperlinks

TODO
